response: {"purpose":"Figure 3f compares the fraction of usable (non-PCR-duplicated) reads among all uniquely mapped reads across three categories of CLIP-based methods: eCLIP, iCLIP (both ENCODE consortium and published datasets), and other published CLIP methods (PAR-CLIP and HITS-CLIP). By displaying individual data points overlaid with kernel density smoothed histograms, the panel allows a direct visual assessment of library complexity and sequencing efficiency across a large number of experiments. This comparison matters because high PCR duplication rates reduce the proportion of informative reads, inflating apparent sequencing depth while providing little additional biological information. Demonstrating that eCLIP achieves a favorable fraction of usable reads relative to existing methods supports its scalability and data quality as a platform for large-scale RBP target identification.","title":"PCR Duplication Rates Compared Across eCLIP, iCLIP, and CLIP Methods"}
